other_observers: Susan Allerton and Barb Byron who arrived before me and had already spotted this bird.; Susan Allerton as noted above.
description: Bird was a medium large wader with a long decurved bill. It did not have any white feathering around the facial skin and the facial skin was a dark grayish-blue bordered by a brighter and lighter blue edge both above and below the facial skin at the base of the bill and next to the gape. The iris was dark with absolutely no reddish highlights. The legs were a dark brown except the taurus joints were reddish. The bill was brownish and warmer toned than the WFIBes it was associating with.Behaviors: It was associating with a large flock of White-faced Ibises that were feeding at the shoreline and into the shallow water. There was also one apparent White-faced x Glossy hybrid that was intermediate in facial skin color and had a glint of reddish in the iris.Call: not heardPlumage: alternate
